data.title: OSL Group will issue the stablecoin USDGO, expected to launch in Q1 next year.
data.description: At the Solana Breakpoint 2025 Summit, Zhang Yinghua, Chief Commercial Officer of OSL Group, announced that OSL Group will launch the USD stablecoin USDGO in partnership with the Solana network. OSL Group will act as the brand and distributor of USDGO, while Anchorage Digital Bank will be the issuer. USDGO is reportedly pegged 1:1 to the US dollar and subject to US federal regulation. It is scheduled for official launch in the first quarter of 2026, with Solana as the first supported blockchain.

data.title: Coinbase selects Chainlink CCIP as its cross-chain bridging infrastructure.
data.description: Coinbase announced that it has selected Chainlink CCIP as its cross-chain bridging infrastructure for its encapsulated assets. The total market capitalization of Coinbase-encapsulated assets, including cbBTC, cbETH, cbDOGE, cbLTC, cbADA, and cbXRP, is approximately $7 billion. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Market Downturn Leads to Partial Liquidation of Ethereum Position
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, HyperInsight monitoring revealed that due to a market downturn, a portion of the Ethereum long position held by 'Maji' was liquidated 8 minutes ago. The liquidation involved 1,200 ETH.

Currently, 'Maji' still holds an Ethereum long position valued at $15.22 million, with an unrealized loss of $420,000. The latest liquidation price was $3,136.

data.title: Market Downturn Leads to Significant Losses for Machi in Ethereum Trading
data.description: According to Foresight News, Onchain Lens monitoring has revealed that Machi Big Brother (@machibigbrother) faced partial liquidation of his 25x leveraged long position in Ethereum due to the market downturn. Eleven hours ago, he closed part of his position at a loss, and the remaining holdings are currently showing an unrealized loss of approximately $480,000. Overall, Machi has incurred losses exceeding $21.6 million.

data.title: The U.S. Senate Banking Committee may postpone its review of the Cryptocurrency Market Structure Act until 2026.
data.description: Crypto journalist Eleanor Terrett wrote on the X platform that US senators from both parties held another meeting on the Cryptocurrency Market Structure Act. After the meeting, Senator Mark Warner revealed that it would be "very difficult" to review the bill next week given its current state, a view shared by other senators. It now appears that the US Senate Banking Committee may have to wait until the new year to review the bill.

data.title: Crypto startup LI.FI raises $29 million, led by Multicoin and CoinFund.
data.description: Crypto startup LI.FI announced the completion of a $29 million funding round, led by Multicoin and CoinFund, bringing its total funding to $52 million. The company has not yet disclosed its latest valuation. LI.FI reportedly plans to use the new funds to expand its business into various trading areas, including perpetual futures, yield opportunities, prediction markets, and lending markets. (Fortune)

data.title: Machi Big Brother's Ethereum long positions are currently showing a floating loss of approximately $480,000, with total losses exceeding $21.6 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, due to the market decline, Machi Dage's 25x leveraged Ethereum long position was partially liquidated. He closed some positions at a loss 11 hours ago, and the current floating loss is $480,000. The total loss is currently over $21.6 million.

data.title: Oaktree Capital Co-founder: Does not believe the Federal Reserve needs to cut interest rates significantly.
data.description: Howard Marks, co-founder of Oaktree Capital Management, warned that the Federal Reserve's "intervention" in the cost of funding will push people towards riskier investments as the returns environment slows. He stated that he does not believe interest rates need to be significantly lower than current levels. Marks said, "I think the Fed should remain passive most of the time, only intervening when the economy is severely overheated, trending towards runaway inflation, or severely depressed and unable to create jobs. I don't think that's the case right now." Earlier this week, Marks wrote in a blog post that he was "terrified" of the impact of artificial intelligence on employment and questioned the practice of hyperscale companies issuing large amounts of debt at extremely low yields to finance AI deployments when the demand for AI remains uncertain. (Jinshi)

data.title: Analysts: The Fed's dovish stance is beneficial to risk assets.
data.description: Following Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell's expressions of concern about the labor market yesterday, Wall Street is increasingly betting that the Fed will cut interest rates twice or more in 2026. Scott Helfstein of Global X stated, "A dovish Fed should generally benefit risk assets, especially growth and cyclical stocks." He also noted, "As borrowing rates fall, sectors related to the corporate investment cycle may also be well-positioned." He added that the attractiveness of short-term bonds and gold may decline slightly. "If short-term rates fall, duration bonds may gain more attention." (Jinshi)

data.title: Howard Marks Warns of Fed's Influence on Investment Risks
data.description: According to Odaily, Howard Marks, co-founder of Oaktree Capital Management, has cautioned that the Federal Reserve's intervention in funding costs could drive investors towards higher-risk investments amid a slowing return environment. Marks expressed skepticism about the necessity for interest rates to be significantly lower than their current levels. He stated, "I believe the Federal Reserve should remain passive most of the time, intervening only when the economy is severely overheating, heading towards rampant inflation, or in a deep slump unable to create jobs. I do not think the current situation warrants such action." Earlier this week, Marks wrote in a blog post about his concerns regarding the impact of artificial intelligence on employment. He also questioned the practice of large-scale enterprises issuing substantial debt at low yields to finance AI deployment, despite uncertain demand for AI.

data.title: The "1011 Insider Whale" has increased its Ethereum long position to over 140,000 ETH, currently showing a floating loss of $4.3 million.
data.description: Hyperbot data shows that after a series of increases in its holdings, "1011 Insider Whale" has reached a position of approximately 140,094 ETH with 5x leverage, currently valued at over $441 million, with a floating loss of approximately $4.3 million and a liquidation price of $2,387.24.

data.title: Grayscale Bittensor Trust Details Announced
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, Grayscale has officially released details about the Grayscale Bittensor Trust. The initial asset management scale for TAO is approximately $10,811,551, with a fee rate of 2.5%. The number of shares in circulation is 1,881,500. This product will be quoted on the OTC Markets Group platform, allowing investors to buy and sell through most traditional brokerage accounts at market-determined prices.

data.title: Jump Crypto Transfers 327 BTC to Anonymous Address
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, data from Arkham indicates that at 22:26, Jump Crypto transferred 327 BTC to an anonymous address starting with 12YGEm.

data.title: Oracle's market value evaporated by over $100 billion
data.description: US stock market data shows that Oracle (ORCL.N) has fallen by 16%, marking its biggest single-day drop since March 2001, wiping out $102 billion in market capitalization. (Jinshi)

data.title: Today, US Bitcoin ETFs saw a net inflow of 3016 BTC, Ethereum ETFs saw a net inflow of 19434 ETH, and SOL ETFs saw a net inflow of 75119 SOL.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, today the US Bitcoin ETF saw a net inflow of 3,016 BTC, with a 7-day net inflow of 1,373 BTC; the Ethereum ETF saw a net inflow of 19,434 ETH, with a 7-day net inflow of 39,594 ETH; and the SOL ETF saw a net inflow of 75,119 SOL, with a 7-day net inflow of 323,438 SOL.

data.title: Bitcoin Treasury Adoption Slows as Major Holders Continue Accumulation
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, the adoption of Bitcoin by treasury companies has significantly slowed in the fourth quarter of 2025. While the largest corporate holders continue to quietly increase their Bitcoin reserves, smaller companies have reduced their activity. Data from blockchain platform CryptoQuant reveals that only nine new companies have added Bitcoin to their balance sheets in the fourth quarter, a sharp decline from the 53 new companies in the previous quarter. In total, 117 new companies have adopted Bitcoin this year.

Despite the overall growth in 2025, most Bitcoin treasury companies hold relatively small amounts. However, the data indicates that well-funded corporate treasuries are still acquiring Bitcoin, even as smaller companies and retail participants reduce their purchases. Some firms have halted their Bitcoin accumulation this quarter, such as Japanese investment company Metaplanet, which has not purchased Bitcoin in over two months. Additionally, some companies are selling their Bitcoin holdings. For instance, UK-based Satsuma Technology sold 579 Bitcoin for approximately $53 million, leaving 620 Bitcoin on its balance sheet.

Amid the slowdown, major corporations continue to accumulate Bitcoin. Strategy, the largest corporate Bitcoin holder, made a significant purchase of $962 million worth of Bitcoin on Monday, marking its largest acquisition since July. This purchase brings the company closer to matching the $21.97 billion worth of Bitcoin it acquired in 2024. Currently, over 1 million Bitcoin, valued at $90.2 billion, is held in public company treasuries, representing 4.7% of the total supply. Additionally, 1.49 million Bitcoin, or 7% of the supply, is held by spot Bitcoin exchange-traded funds.

The slowdown in digital asset treasury (DAT) acquisitions is also evident. Ripple-backed Evernorth Holdings has been inactive since the end of October, following its acquisition of $950 million in XRP tokens. Evernorth's XRP holdings have faced nearly $80 million in unrealized losses due to market declines and increasing pressure on DATs. BitMine Immersion Technologies, the largest corporate Ether holder, has also reduced its Ether acquisitions from a peak of $2.6 billion in July to $296 million in December. Cumulative investments from Ether treasury firms have decreased by 81% over the past three months, from 1.97 million ETH acquired in August to 370,000 ETH in November.

data.title: Grayscale discloses GTAO details: fee rate 2.5%, initial TAO assets under management exceed US$10 million.
data.description: Grayscale officially announced details of the Grayscale Bittensor Trust, with an initial TAO asset management scale of approximately $10,811,551, a fee rate of 2.5%, and an outstanding number of 1,881,500 units. The product will be quoted on the OTC Markets Group platform, and investors can buy and sell it at market-determined prices through most traditional brokerage accounts.

data.title: Binance Alpha lists CYS
data.description: According to the official page, Binance Alpha has listed CYS. Contract address: 0x0c69199c1562233640e0db5ce2c399a88eb507c7

data.title: Swedish fintech company Klarna plans to develop a crypto wallet.
data.description: Swedish fintech company Klarna has announced a partnership with Privy, Stripe's wallet infrastructure platform, to develop a mainstream crypto wallet. Klarna stated that the wallet project is still in the exploratory stage, and the final product launch is subject to regulatory approval. (The Block)

data.title: Republic Technologies increased its holdings by 742.4 ETH, bringing its total holdings to 1,570.6 ETH.
data.description: Canadian publicly traded company Republic Technologies disclosed that it has increased its holdings of ETH by 742.4, bringing its total holdings to 1,570.6, at an average acquisition price of $2,700 per coin, with a current market value of approximately $5.27 million. Republic stated that it will use ETH as an operating asset and has drawn $10 million from a previous $100 million secured convertible note financing facility to further increase its ETH holdings. (Businesswire)
